children.text: the appropriation thereof has been previously authorized by law enacted on or after ; orJuly 12, 1985
the amount of all such obligations and expenditures does not exceed an amount previously prescribed by law enacted on or after such date.

children.heading: Exception for later legislation authorizing obligations or expenditures
children.label: b Exception for later legislation authorizing obligations or expenditures
children.text: To the extent that legislation enacted after the making of an appropriation to carry out any export promotion program authorizes the obligation or expenditure thereof, the limitation contained in subsection (a) shall have no effect.

children.heading: Provisions must be specifically superseded
children.label: c Provisions must be specifically superseded
children.text: The provisions of this section shall not be superseded except by a provision of law enacted after , which specifically repeals, modifies, or supersedes the provisions of this section.July 12, 1985

trade development (except for the trade adjustment assistance program) and dissemination of foreign marketing opportunities and other marketing information to United States producers of goods and services, including the expansion of foreign markets for United States textiles and apparel and any other United States products;

the development of regional and multilateral economic policies which enhance United States trade and investment interests, and the provision of marketing services with respect to foreign countries and regions;
the exhibition of United States goods in other countries;
the operations of the United States and Foreign Commercial Service, or any successor agency; and
the Market Development Cooperator Program established under , and assistance for trade shows provided under .section 4723 of this titlesection 4724 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the printing, distribution, and sale of documents outside the contiguous United States, if the Secretary finds that the implementation of such export promotion program would be more efficient, and if such documents will be distributed primarily and sold exclusively outside the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: the acceptance of private notices and advertisements in connection with the printing and distribution of such documents.

Any fees received by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be deposited in a separate account or accounts which may be used to defray directly the costs incurred in conducting activities authorized by paragraph (1) or to repay or make advances to appropriations or other funds available for such activities.
